title: Fix skipping tests in test container
body:
Without this env. var. being set from hack/make.sh, many/most
integration tests will `SKIP`.  Fix this by notifying the user
and setting the magic `SKOPEO_CONTAINER_TESTS` variable.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@vrothberg @mtrmac FYI- I'm not going to fix that Travis test failure in this PR...it's a docker-hub rate-limit issue (surprise!).
Ref: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1215

LGTM
other than the rate limit hiccup in the test.
We really need to move the CI away from using Docker Hub.

> We really need to move the CI away from using Docker Hub.

Yes, I predict it will be come **worse** under Cirrus-CI (faster running tests, people will run tests more).  I started looking into this myself but quickly got overwhelmed.  Someone familiar with the tests should take on this work.

I restarted the Travis tests that failed yesterday, hopefully the rate-limit-$DEITY will be kind.
Thank you @cevich!  We can do the migration to Quay once the Cirrus work is done.  I was hesitant to do that simultaneously as I was afraid of too many merge conflicts.
